name: mem-search
tagline: Guides Claude through a search -> timeline -> fetch workflow against claude-mem's MCP memory tools to recall past session work.
verdict: Clean, read-only skill: it only calls three documented MCP tools (search, timeline, get_observations) to look up prior session records, with no shell exec, network egress beyond those tools, secrets access, or hidden instructions. Minor gaps: no guidance for empty/failed search results (Q06) and no version/change marker (H08), so updates or breakage are hard

highlights.summary: No guidance for empty search results, invalid/missing anchor IDs, or MCP tool errors; a user relying on this skill gets no fallback behavior documented if a step fails.

The skill depends on three specific MCP tools (search, timeline, get_observations) referenced only in prose, not declared as a formal tool/dependency list in frontmatter, so a user can't tell at a gla
No version field or changelog/date marker in frontmatter or body, so users can't tell if the copy they installed is current or stale relative to the upstream repo.
